programmes.show_description: The Inspector's Pets: Enduring '70s British sitcom about a chirpy bus driver. Stan and Jack are coerced into looking after the inspector's pets when he goes on holiday with his mum.
Your daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection.
Shopping from home.
A Car by Any Other Name: Classic British sitcom. The vicar wants to sell his car and gets Simon to help, but confusion soon reigns when Oliver tries to sell his car at the same time.
The Epidemic: Enduring '70s British sitcom about a chirpy driver and his conductor pal at the Luxton Bus Company. Can Stan avoid the dreaded 'lurgy' before a big date?
Jo has a go at Andy for taking his temper out on Sarah's new dolls house. Meanwhile, Greg tells Mel that he's found a buyer for the house and suggests they move to Spain.
Ashley turns to Mel for help with Arthur. Scarlett plans a trip to Canada to spend time with her mum - leaving Lexi feeling alone - and Andy apologises for losing his temper.
David threatens to turn himself in after admitting to a horrified Gail he pushed her and left her for dead, while Paul feels the strain as the impact of the arson attack sinks in.
The residents are agog as David, hell-bent on destruction and determined to get sent down, causes chaos. Leanne tries to persuade Dan to give her a job in the bookies.
Hidden Truth: When a vital witness to a gangland killing goes missing before she can give evidence in court, Frost is under pressure to find her and her son before they are killed.
Changing Places: The very first episode of the drama series. A policeman has moved north from London full of hopes and expectations, but both he and his wife face local hostility.
Fruits of the Earth: Drama set in North Yorkshire. Nick pursues a moorland flasher and drunken hikers, and Kate encounters abuse when she helps a woman with a bullying husband.
Bad Tidings: Murder mystery drama. Sergeant Dan Scott arrives to work with Barnaby - and is thrown straight into a murder investigation in Midsomer Mallow.
Greg is annoyed to discover Mel has visited Arthur. Miles becomes jealous when he sees Jake and Jasmine getting along at the Woolpack, and Nicola spies an opportunity at work.
Jake begins his trial run at the Woolpack, where he and Jasmine start to play pranks on each other. Nicola finds more interest in her services, and Ashley suggests a family picnic.
After spending the night in a prison cell, David comes clean about his past misdeeds, but Gail refuses to testify against him. Becky takes one of the builders back to Roy's place.
Gail intervenes during the court proceedings as David calmly accepts his fate and insists he is guilty on all counts. Roy reaches a compromise with Becky over her promiscuity.
Rumours: Drama set in the North York Moors. Nick gets an anonymous tip-off about a pop star's undesirable predilections, and Greengrass is in trouble because of village gossip.
Playing with Fire: Drama set in North Yorkshire. Burning desires of all kinds bring problems for Nick and Kate, and a wandering horse and wandering salmon also cause frustration.
Happily Ever After: Comedy-drama series about a doctor who moves to a small Cornish town. Martin and Louisa's wedding day arrives, but the day is plagued by all sorts of disasters.
Better the Devil: Comedy-drama series. Martin considers leaving Portwenn after he meets an old flame who is now a top doctor, but Louisa then returns with some big news of her own.
Neverland: Crime drama series. Endeavour and Thursday must overcome corruption in Oxford's corridors of power when the death of a journalist threatens to expose past horrors.
Hidden Truth: When a vital witness to a gangland killing goes missing before she can give evidence in court, Frost is under pressure to find her and her son before they are killed.
The Epidemic: Enduring '70s British sitcom about a chirpy driver and his conductor pal at the Luxton Bus Company. Can Stan avoid the dreaded 'lurgy' before a big date?
Your daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection.
Shopping from home.
Dead on Arrival: Drama series about a Cornish detective. Wycliffe's silver wedding anniversary celebrations come to an abrupt halt when crime rears its head.
Number of the Beast: Drama series about a Cornish police detective. When unexplained killings rock the Bodmin community, locals are convinced that the 'beast' is back.
Falling Darkness: Lewis and Hathaway investigate the murder of a university friend of Hobson - as more deaths occur, they realise that Laura's past may hold the key.
The Body in the Library: Miss Marple is called by an old friend to help discover who strangled a young dancer in the library of Gossington Hall.
The Invisible Worm: Light-hearted drama series. Rosemary and Laura are caught up in a strange ritual when they visit a preparatory school to investigate why its roses are dying.
The Gongoozlers: Light-hearted drama series. Laura teams up with a yachtswoman-turned-TV presenter to investigate when Rosemary is injured in a suspicious filming accident.
Dead in the Water: Barnaby and Scott dig into the luxury lifestyles of the rowing set when the annual Midsomer Regatta is brought to a standstill after a body is found in a river.
Orchis Fatalis: When one of the world's rarest orchids is smuggled illegally into Midsomer Malham, it triggers a catalogue of passion, jealousy and death.
Second Sight: When Barnaby and Scott investigate the death of a man recently ejected from a pub for fighting, the post-mortem shows that he may have been subject to electric shocks.
Hidden Depths: Barnaby and Scott investigate when a solicitor falls from a roof to his death. A revenge motive emerges when the dead man's rival also dies - but who is behind it?
The Seagull: DCI Vera Stanhope must find the connection between a skeleton discovered underneath the site of a notorious burnt-out nightclub and a present-day murder.
Near Death Experience: Frost begins a manhunt for a serial killer when he uncovers a web of lies surrounding the investigation into the vicious murder of a widow.
